Container.Name: iGPU-Stream-Benchmark
Container.Overview: Measures the maximum SUSTAINED number of simultaneous 4K HEVC -> 1080p H.264 (8 Mbit, strict >= 1.0x realtime) transcodes a GPU can do, shown live on a web scoreboard you can watch in a browser. Supports Intel (VAAPI), AMD (VAAPI) and NVIDIA (NVENC/NVDEC) - you pick which GPU in the system to test from the web UI. It ramps the stream count one at a time and reports the highest count where EVERY stream still runs at or above real-time (same workload across vendors, so the numbers are comparable). Built on the Jellyfin image only to reuse the jellyfin-ffmpeg binary and its validated driver stack - the Jellyfin server itself is NOT run (the entrypoint is overridden). This is a run-and-watch benchmark, not an always-on service: pick a GPU, start it, watch the scoreboard reach the limit, then stop it. Nothing else should be using the GPU while it runs, or the result will be skewed. GPU PASSTHROUGH: this template works out-of-the-box for INTEL and AMD via the /dev/dri device below. FOR NVIDIA you MUST add --runtime=nvidia to Extra Parameters (the NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES / NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES vars are already set, and the NVIDIA driver plugin must be installed) - on an NVIDIA-only host you can also remove the /dev/dri device. NOTE: the image is currently LOCAL-ONLY on this server and has not been pushed to Docker Hub yet - run it from the local image and do NOT use Unraid's force-update (it will fail to pull until the image is pushed).
Container.Requires: A GPU with hardware transcoding. INTEL / AMD: works out of the box — just keep the /dev/dri device (VAAPI). NVIDIA: you MUST add --runtime=nvidia to Extra Parameters (it is NOT included by default; the NVIDIA driver plugin is required); the /dev/dri device can stay if you also have an Intel/AMD GPU, or be removed on NVIDIA-only hosts. --cap-add PERFMON (in Extra Parameters by default) lets intel_gpu_top read the Intel iGPU live telemetry (engine %, clock). OPTIONAL: add --pid=host to Extra Parameters to NAME which apps are using a GPU in the pre-test busy check (read-only host process visibility) - without it the check still warns when a GPU is busy, it just can't name the app. The RAM-speed readout (Intel iGPU) needs the read-only DMI mount below. The benchmark runs fine without these extras.

Config.Name: WebUI
Config.Description: Scoreboard web UI - open in a browser to pick a GPU and watch the benchmark live.

Config.Name: GPU device (Intel / AMD)
Config.Description: Intel/AMD GPU passthrough (VAAPI) - required to test an Intel or AMD GPU. Leave it for any host that has an Intel or AMD GPU. On an NVIDIA-only host with no /dev/dri you can remove this and instead add --runtime=nvidia to Extra Parameters.

Config.Name: RAM disk (source clip)
Config.Description: MUST be a RAM-backed path. The benchmark hot-loads its source clip here at run start; keeping it in RAM ensures we measure the GPU, not disk I/O. /tmp is RAM-backed on Unraid - do NOT point this at an array or cache disk, or the stream count will be limited by storage speed instead of the GPU.

Config.Name: App data (CPU baseline)
Config.Description: Persistent app-data folder (standard Unraid appdata). Stores your CPU efficiency baseline (cpu_baseline.json): run the 'CPU - software' device once and every later GPU run of the same profile shows how many times faster and more power-efficient the GPU is than plain CPU transcoding. Safe to leave as-is.

Config.Name: CPU power readout (optional)
Config.Description: Optional, read-only. Exposes the kernel's RAPL energy counters (the same source as the Unraid dashboard's CPU power) so CPU and iGPU tests can show real package watts - works on Intel AND modern AMD (Zen 2+) CPUs, no extra capabilities needed. Safe to leave as-is; remove it and CPU power falls back to intel_gpu_top (Intel GPU + PERFMON only).

Config.Name: RAM speed readout (Intel iGPU, optional)
Config.Description: Optional, read-only. Lets the scoreboard show the system RAM type/speed for Intel iGPU runs (useful for explaining different results - e.g. XMP/EXPO off). Safe to leave as-is; remove it if you prefer not to expose the DMI tables.

Config.Name: Unraid version (optional)
Config.Description: Optional, read-only. Lets the result include your Unraid OS version (handy for the shared results database). Safe to leave as-is.

Config.Name: SUBMIT_URL
Config.Description: Advanced. Leaderboard endpoint override - the built-in default submits to the official shared leaderboard. Set to a single space to keep all results local-only (the Submit button disappears).

Config.Name: Your test clips folder
Config.Description: Read-only drop folder for benchmarking YOUR OWN videos: copy one or a few files in and they appear in the 'Or test your own file' picker (codec and resolution auto-detected). The benchmark samples 60 seconds from the MIDDLE of the chosen file (openings are easy-to-decode logos/credits) and loads it into RAM. You can point this at any folder - but NOT your whole media library (too many files and it refuses). Custom-file runs are local-only and never submitted to the shared database.

Config.Name: HOLD_SECONDS
Config.Description: Seconds each stream-count level is held at steady state before the pass/fail decision (startup jitter is discarded first).

Config.Name: PASS_THRESHOLD
Config.Description: Minimum x-realtime speed EVERY stream must sustain to count as a pass. Default 1.0 = strict real-time. Lowering it (e.g. 0.95) allows streams that run slightly below real-time to count.

Config.Name: MAX_STREAMS
Config.Description: Safety cap on the ramp. Fast GPUs (e.g. high-end NVIDIA) can sustain many streams; raise only if a run ends with 'MAX >= 128'.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
Config.Description: NVIDIA only. Which NVIDIA GPUs the container can see. 'all' exposes every NVIDIA GPU. Requires --runtime=nvidia in Extra Parameters. Ignored by Intel/AMD.

Config.Name: NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES
Config.Description: NVIDIA only. Driver capabilities exposed to the container. 'all' includes video (NVENC/NVDEC) + compute. Requires --runtime=nvidia in Extra Parameters. Ignored by Intel/AMD.
